certifications.title: Core certifications
certifications.subtitle: I continuously improve my skills through focused learning paths and technical certifications.

hero: I work as a Software Developer and Delivery Expert at Lectra, where I focus on developing, optimizing, and tailoring software solutions for complex fashion-tech contexts.
I implement new features, optimize existing ones, and fix application behaviors, with constant attention to customer needs and product quality.

The product I work on, Kubix Link, spans front-end, back-end, databases, APIs, external service integrations, and custom code for specific client needs. This experience gave me a broad, practical view of what it takes to build and maintain a complex web application.

contact.title: Contact me
contact.subtitle: If you want to discuss a collaboration, a project, or a new professional opportunity, feel free to reach out. I review every message carefully.
contact.emailHelper: Primary channel for projects and collaborations. If possible, include your goal and context in the message.
contact.linkedinDescription: For professional networking, quick updates, and direct outreach.
contact.resumeDescription: Latest version of my profile with experience, stack, and skills.

bio.title: Bio
bio.description: I’m Tommaso Berti, a software developer with a strong passion for technology and building useful tools. I enjoy tackling complex problems, finding practical solutions, and continuously improving how I work. In my day-to-day work, I combine curiosity, analytical thinking, and pragmatism, both when developing new features and when optimizing existing systems.

hobbies.title: Hobbies
hobbies.description1: I have played volleyball for many years, and it has taught me discipline, teamwork, and pressure management. As a setter, I need to make quick and reasoned decisions during play while staying focused, even in intense moments.
hobbies.description2: I’m also passionate about gaming and technology. Through games, I’ve explored creative worlds and stories that continue to fuel my curiosity and imagination. I enjoy traveling, seeking new experiences, and connecting with different people, because strong interpersonal exchange helps me grow both personally and professionally.

experiences.title: High School Diploma (Scientific Lyceum)
experiences.description: I completed my Scientific High School studies, gaining solid foundations in mathematics, physics, and computer science. I participated in various school projects that strengthened my analytical thinking and problem-solving abilities. This school experience taught me methodological discipline and how to approach complex topics with structure.

experiences.title: Computer Engineering University in Padua (Veneto, Italy)
experiences.description: During my first two university years in Padua, I studied the fundamentals of computer engineering and algorithm design. I worked on practical labs and collaborated with classmates on programming projects. This period gave me a strong academic base and teamwork skills applied to software development.

experiences.title: Computer university in Venice (Veneto, Italy)
experiences.description: Continuing my studies in Venice, I focused on system architectures, databases, and web development. I took part in academic projects that involved building applications and experimenting with modern technologies. This experience strengthened my independence and adaptability.

experiences.title: Art.Ap
experiences.description: I worked as a guide during two temporary exhibitions and managed the organization’s official website. My tasks included visitor assistance, content updates, and coordination with the exhibition team.

experiences.title: DeFra Web - Contractor
experiences.description: I managed web content using CMS platforms and handled periodic backups. I also worked on catalog updates, migrations, client meetings, documentation, and PHP/CSS/HTML adjustments.

experiences.title: Kubix Link (A Lectra Company) - Full time employee
experiences.description: I contribute to digital solutions for PLM/PIM in the fashion industry. My role involves cross-team collaboration and cloud-based data processes.

certifications.title: Certifications
certifications.subtitle: Continuous learning path with certifications grouped by study area.

projects.title: Projects
projects.description: This project collection reflects my passion for building useful tools, exploring new technologies, and continuously improving my skills. Some projects start from curiosity, others from the need to optimize my workflow or test new ideas. In my software development work, I use AI as practical support: it helps accelerate specific phases, improve code quality, and validate new solutions quickly, always with critical review of the output.
projects.load_preview_tooltip: The preview loads only on click to avoid unnecessary API calls.

exercises.title: Exercises
exercises.subtitle: Selected GitHub starred repositories I use as practical exercises and learning references.

react.label: React
react.description: Built the application using React’s component-based architecture, enabling clean structure, reusability, and efficient UI updates.

vite.label: Vite
vite.description: Build and dev tool selected for fast startup, efficient hot reload, and a lean frontend pipeline.

react-router.label: React Router
react-router.description: Frontend routing library used to manage client-side navigation and page flows predictably.

mui.label: MUI
mui.description: Leveraged MUI’s React components and theming capabilities to rapidly develop a clean and accessible user interface.

javascript.label: JavaScript
javascript.description: Developed the application logic using modern JavaScript features, ensuring clean, maintainable, and efficient code.

nodejs.label: Node.js
nodejs.description: Backend runtime used to build modular APIs and manage authentication, sessions, and server-side business logic.

express.label: Express
express.description: Backend framework used to build REST endpoints and organize middleware in a modular way.

passport.label: Passport
passport.description: Authentication library integrated to handle local login and OAuth in an extensible way.

express-session.label: express-session
express-session.description: Session middleware used to persist authenticated user state server-side in a secure and consistent way.

postgresql.label: PostgreSQL
postgresql.description: Relational database chosen for reliability, data integrity, and structured queries across products, carts, and orders.

github.label: GitHub
github.description: Integrated GitHub for version control and automated workflows through GitHub Actions.

github-actions.label: GitHub Actions
github-actions.description: CI/CD pipeline used for versioned frontend and backend build/deploy workflows.

swagger-openapi.label: Swagger / OpenAPI
swagger-openapi.description: API documentation and testing layer used to make endpoints and payloads immediately verifiable.

webstorm.label: WebStorm
webstorm.description: Used WebStorm as the main IDE, leveraging its advanced code analysis, refactoring tools, and integrated development workflows.

caddy.label: Caddy
caddy.description: Used Caddy as a modern web server and reverse proxy, leveraging automatic HTTPS and simple configuration for fast, reliable deployment.

minisearch.label: Minisearch
minisearch.description: Lightweight full-text search integration that enables fast client-side indexing and real-time query results.

tailwindcss.label: TailwindCSS
tailwindcss.description: Utility-first CSS framework used to build responsive and consistent interfaces with minimal custom styling. I used it to experiment with the library.

css.label: CSS
css.description: Styling language used to design some layouts, control visual appearance, and ensure responsive presentation across devices.

codex.label: Codex
codex.description: AI development tool used in my workflow to speed up repetitive tasks, explore solutions, and improve productivity.

codexpane.title: CodexPane
codexpane.description: Web platform designed to speed up technical study and reference work by combining curated documentation, practical examples, cheatsheets, and interactive code playgrounds in a fast, focused interface.

introduction.title: CodexPane
description: CodexPane started from a practical workflow issue: while studying and developing, I was losing time switching between documentation tabs, snippets, and examples. I built the app as a focused workspace for fast access to high-quality technical references.

The platform combines curated documentation, practical examples, cheatsheets, and interactive playgrounds in a single flow. The goal is to reduce context switching and speed up the path from search to implementation.

search_mechanics: Content is indexed client-side with MiniSearch, so lookups stay fast without relying on external search services.
Queries run across section titles and text blocks, returning relevance-ordered results that are easy to scan.
Data is structured to balance search speed, result quality, and long-term maintainability.
The search UX is designed to minimize time between a technical question and concrete implementation.

react.description: React is the core UI layer: chosen for componentization and reuse, it keeps documentation, examples, and playground areas modular and consistent.

javascript.description: JavaScript handles navigation, search behavior, and content composition logic while keeping the codebase readable and extensible.

mui.description: MUI provides an accessible and consistent UI baseline, helping maintain visual coherence and speed up component development.

tailwindcss.description: Tailwind CSS is used selectively for utility-driven layout control and quick styling refinements where fine-grained tuning was needed.

minisearch.description: MiniSearch is the client-side search engine: it indexes technical content and returns fast results without server-side dependencies, improving lookup speed and UX.

caddy.description: Caddy covers deployment as web server and reverse proxy, with automatic HTTPS and a lean configuration for reliable releases.

github.description: GitHub is used for version control, collaboration, and release automation, keeping changes traceable and the development cycle structured.

webstorm.description: WebStorm is the primary IDE: static analysis and refactoring tools help preserve code quality and consistency.

css.description: CSS is used for targeted visual customizations and fine control of style details not fully covered by utility classes.

codex.description: Codex/ChatGPT was used as technical support to accelerate iterations, compare approaches, and refine implementations with critical review.

difficulties_faced: Learn how to use MUI components and theming system to create a consistent and accessible user interface
Implementing an efficient client-side search functionality that can handle large documentation datasets without compromising performance.
Designing an intuitive and user-friendly interface that allows users to quickly find and navigate through various documentation sections and examples.
Ensuring the accuracy and relevance of the documentation content, which involved curating and organizing information from multiple sources.
Understand the right balance between features and simplicity, avoiding feature bloat while still providing valuable tools like code playgrounds and cheatsheets.
Define a clear project folder hierarchy and naming convention to easily manage and scale the documentation content as new languages and frameworks are added.
Implementing dynamic routing for project detail pages, allowing easy navigation across different topics, sections and subsections.
Introducing a search feature that enhance the discoverability of relevant information.

v1.title: v1.0.0
content: First version including core documentation search, basic examples and the initial UI.
Hosting on VPS
Automate the webapp release with GitHub workflow, with automatic versioning system

v2.title: v2.0.0
content: Introduce new documentation categories to improve content organization.
Refine the structure of subsections by strengthening the relationship between IDs and titles.
Enhance visual consistency across all interactive code playgrounds to improve usability and performance.
Add additional code playgrounds to expand the number of interactive examples.
Improve the search feature by indexing section and subsection content and dynamically highlighting matched text (MUI-style).

v3.title: v3.0.0
content: Create a contributor guide to help new contributors understand how to participate in the project.
Display contributor avatars on related documents, sections, and subsections to credit authorship.

v4.title: v4.0.0
content: Add cheatsheet-style documentation with options to download or copy content easily.
Integrate a simple AI chatbot, powered by the existing documentation, to help users find the right information and provide well-formatted responses.

ecommerce-rest-api.title: E-commerce REST API
ecommerce-rest-api.description: Full-stack e-commerce demo with React frontend, Express API, and PostgreSQL, designed for a fast development workflow and structured VPS deployment.

introduction.title: E-commerce REST API
description: This project was built to simulate a real full-stack scenario, combining a React frontend and Express API under the same domain, with backend traffic securely proxied through `/api`.
The goal was to deliver a complete and credible engineering flow: session-based auth, cart/order management, Swagger API documentation, and versioned VPS deployment pipelines.

react.description: React drives the catalog UI and core user flows with modular components designed for fast extension of pages and states.

vite.description: Vite was chosen to speed up frontend development and builds, reducing iteration time while working on UI and user flows.

react-router.description: React Router handles transitions between client views and application states, keeping navigation clear and predictable.

javascript.description: JavaScript is used on both client and server to keep technical consistency across API logic, UI behavior, and shared patterns.

mui.description: MUI provides accessible and consistent components for the main UI surfaces, reducing implementation time while preserving visual coherence.

tailwindcss.description: Tailwind CSS is used for utility-first layout control and quick style refinements where fine-grained tuning was needed.

nodejs.description: Node.js is the Express runtime layer, handling API routing, authentication, sessions, and business logic orchestration.

express.description: Express structures the app REST services with dedicated middleware for validation, auth, and error handling.

passport.description: Passport was adopted to keep authentication modular (local + optional Google OAuth) without coupling auth logic to endpoint handlers.

express-session.description: express-session manages persistence of authenticated user sessions across requests, a key requirement for protected routes like orders and checkout.

postgresql.description: PostgreSQL is the project data layer, used to persist users, products, carts, and orders through a clear relational schema.

caddy.description: Caddy manages reverse proxy and automatic HTTPS, serving frontend and backend under one domain with a clean deployment setup.

github.description: GitHub centralizes version control and CI/CD through separate frontend/backend workflows with versioned releases and symlink activation.

github-actions.description: GitHub Actions automates build and deployment of both application layers, reducing manual errors and improving release repeatability.

swagger-openapi.description: Swagger/OpenAPI is used to document and test APIs transparently, improving technical verification and onboarding.

webstorm.description: WebStorm was the primary IDE for refactoring, debugging, and maintaining code quality in the monorepo workflow.

codex.description: Codex/ChatGPT supported deployment automation and technical documentation work, with manual validation of architecture decisions.

difficulties_faced: Designing robust session-based authentication (local login + optional Google OAuth) while keeping the user flow consistent.
Handling frontend/API routing through a single domain proxy without exposing backend services directly.
Building a reliable VPS deployment pipeline with versioned releases and controlled activation.
Managing demo database reset on backend deploy without compromising runtime stability and functional checks.
Documenting APIs and payloads clearly to keep the project immediately verifiable for recruiters and developers.

v1.title: v1.0.0
content: Monorepo setup with React/Vite frontend, Express backend, and initial PostgreSQL schema.
Implementation of core APIs (products, cart, checkout, local authentication).
VPS deployment with Caddy and systemd services, with backend exposed only via `/api`.

v2.title: v2.0.0
content: Refine authentication/session flows, including optional Google OAuth integration.
Improve API input validation and server-side error handling coverage.
Enhance operational observability (health checks, logging, and deploy troubleshooting).

v3.title: v3.0.0
content: Extend automated end-to-end tests for critical flows (auth, cart, checkout).
Strengthen technical documentation for faster contributor onboarding.
Introduce baseline API performance metrics and response-time monitoring.

portfolio.title: Portfolio
portfolio.description: Personal portfolio built to present my work, experience, and projects in a clear, modern, and accessible way.

introduction.title: Portfolio
description: This portfolio was designed as a modular React application, with reusable components and dynamic routing to keep the codebase organized, scalable, and easy to evolve.

The interface is built with a strong focus on accessibility, readability, and visual consistency; it includes i18n support, light/dark theme handling, and a technical foundation built to extend new sections without unnecessary complexity.

lessons_learned: Designing reusable components with clear responsibilities improves maintainability and development speed.
Integrating i18n early reduces future extension costs and keeps content/UI consistency high.
Balancing flexibility and simplicity is essential: every new feature should deliver concrete value.
Automating build and deploy flows improves reliability and helps maintain consistent quality over time.

react.description: React is the UI foundation: chosen for componentization and reuse, it enabled a clear page structure and scalable composition of interface blocks.

javascript.description: JavaScript powers the application logic, from dynamic rendering to section composition, while keeping the codebase readable and maintainable.

mui.description: MUI was used to build accessible and consistent components quickly, with a visual baseline that is easy to maintain and extend.

caddy.description: Caddy supports deployment as web server and reverse proxy, with lean configuration and automatic HTTPS for reliable publishing.

github.description: GitHub is used for version control and collaboration, with clear workflows to track changes and releases in a structured way.

webstorm.description: WebStorm is the primary IDE: refactoring and code analysis tools improved code quality and day-to-day development productivity.

codex.description: Codex/ChatGPT was used as technical support to speed up iterations, validate approaches, and refine implementations, always with critical review.

difficulties_faced: Learn how to use MUI components and theming system to create a consistent and accessible user interface
Setting up internationalization (i18n) to support multiple languages and ensure seamless content translation across the site.
Implementing dynamic routing for project detail pages, allowing easy navigation
Creation of Custom Icon components to maintain a consistent visual style and improve performance.
Creation of a custom Breadcrumb component with terminal-style navigation, including the ability to use `..` to move to the parent category.

v1.title: v1.0.0
content: Initial release featuring pages, navigation, and a dark/light theme system, with a basic GUI and UX.
Hosting on VPS
Automate the webapp release with GitHub workflow, with automatic versioning system
Localization management with English and Italian translations.
Advanced terminal-style breadcrumb navigation, including the ability to use `..` to move to the parent category.
MiniWebApp preview to enable quick interaction with the project.
A dedicated page for each main project, showcasing its goals, challenges faced, technologies used, and roadmap.

v2.title: v2.0.0
content: Improve user experience through smoother animations and a more consistent visual design.
Implement dynamic URL updates on the About page to let users navigate directly to specific sections while scrolling.
Add a status bar to each project page showing the latest commit (via GitHub APIs) along with key project metrics such as code composition and number of commits.
Show a preview of my GitHub profile on the portfolio homepage, including pinned repositories, recent activity, and relevant highlights.

v3.title: v3.0.0
content: Develop a custom Blog feature for publishing articles, including categories, tags, external links, and rich media support.
Integrate Redux to handle advanced state management and improve the efficiency of all database CRUD operations.
Adopt a MongoDB database to securely store, query, and manage all blog posts.
